purpose.summary: A visual node-based IDE for designing, training, and tuning GENREG (Genetic Regulatory) AI models
purpose.description: Genome Studio provides a LiteGraph-based interface to construct and visualize AI architectures that combine evolutionary algorithms with regulatory protein networks. The system uses Snake as a testbed environment, but the architecture is designed to be environment-agnostic for future applications.
goals: Visualize AI model architecture as connected node graphs
Real-time monitoring of training metrics (trust, fitness, population stats)
Interactive parameter tuning without code changes
Separation of regulatory layer (proteins) from functional layer (controller)

frontend.role: Visual graph editor, monitoring, user control

genreg_model.concept: Two-layer AI architecture inspired by biological gene regulation

regulatory.name: Protein Network
regulatory.purpose: Process environmental signals into trust modifiers

functional.name: Controller Network
functional.purpose: Select actions based on input signals
functional.architecture: Feed-forward neural network (11 inputs -> 16 hidden -> 4 outputs)

evolution.mutation: Gaussian noise on weights and protein parameters

steps: 1. Genome Builder creates population of N genomes
2. Episode Runner starts episode for genome[0]
8. When snake dies, Episode Runner finalizes, advances to next genome
9. After all genomes evaluated, Generation Manager triggers evolution
10. New population feeds back to Genome Builder, repeat

future_plans: Support for multiple environment types beyond Snake
More protein types for complex regulatory networks

hopf_subsystem.status: experimental, separate from GenoFlow Snake IDE
hopf_subsystem.summary: Closed-form kernel-ridge classifier on fixed geometric features derived from the 600-cell ADE eigenspace decomposition, the Hopf fibration S^3 -> S^2, and the discrete de Rham ladder up through Omega^3 cell forms.
hopf_subsystem.best_result: 97.39% MNIST test accuracy at v10 with multi-scale polynomial kernel ridge over 879 fixed features
hopf_subsystem.training_is: single closed-form (kernel) ridge solve, zero non-convex parameters
hopf_subsystem.not_a_learned_controller: the feature extractor is fixed geometry; only the readout is fit

version_progression.v9: 293 features (+ curl Omega^1 + E8 edges), linear ridge, 96.12%
version_progression.v10: 879 features (v9 x 3 kappas), poly kernel ridge Nystrom m=5000, 97.39%
version_progression.v11: 1179 features (v10 + face Omega^2 + triangle Berry), poly kernel ridge, 97.32% (no MNIST improvement over v10)
version_progression.v12: 1611 features (v11 + co-exact Omega^2 + cell Omega^3 via d2), poly kernel ridge, 97.24% (no MNIST improvement over v10)

core_files: hopf_controller.py - geometric primitives and controller classes (v6-v9), plus face/triangle/cell machinery used by v11/v12
ade_geometry.py - ADE-structured spectral decomposition with CG cross products
train_v11.py - v11 trainer (adds Omega^2 face eigenspaces and triangle Berry)
train_v12.py - v12 trainer (adds Omega^3 cell eigenspaces via d2)
hopf_subsystem.retractions: An earlier interpretation of the signed Berry phase (holonomy_triangle in hopf_controller.py, extended by v11 triangle_berry and v12 cell chirality weights) as a chirality-specific causal signal is not supported by rigorous cross-digit transfer comparisons. See FINDINGS.md for the full tables and the explicit retraction.
see_also: FINDINGS.md - honest results, retractions, documented arbitrary choices
PRE_REGISTRATION.md - pre-registered next test for the multi-stage Hopf architecture that has not yet been built
